PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
CT-guided percutaneous biopsy of the right lower lobe mass reveals moderately to poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma, solid predominant pattern with focal mucinous features. TTF-1 positive, Napsin A weakly positive, CK7 positive, p40 negative. Tumor invades visceral pleura. Adequate tissue submitted for molecular testing.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
Comprehensive genomic profiling (FoundationOne CDx) on right lower lobe biopsy. EGFR exon 21 L858R mutation detected (VAF 32%). TP53 G245S mutation (VAF 30%). RB1 splice site mutation (VAF 15%). No ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MET, RET, or KRAS alterations. PD-L1 TPS 15%. TMB 5 mutations/Mb. Microsatellite stable.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant adenocarcinoma with chest wall invasion. Unresectable. Concurrent chemoradiation is standard for stage III, but presence of EGFR driver mutation favors TKI-based approach. Plan: Per LAURA trial data, initiate concurrent chemoradiation followed by consolidation osimertinib. Discuss with radiation oncology.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis after completion of chemoradiation shows decrease in right lower lobe mass from 5.8 cm to 3.2 cm. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy decreased. No distant metastases. Partial response.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: Good response to concurrent chemoradiation. Recovered well from treatment. ECOG 1. Plan: Start consolidation osimertinib 80 mg daily per LAURA trial paradigm for EGFR-mutant Stage III NSCLC after chemoradiation.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
Restaging CT at 3 months on consolidation osimertinib shows stable residual right lower lobe lesion measuring 2.8 cm. Post-radiation fibrotic changes noted. No new disease.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 6 months shows continued stability. Right lower lobe residual 2.6 cm with surrounding fibrosis. No evidence of progression or distant metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
MRI brain at 6 months shows no intracranial metastatic disease.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: Stable disease on consolidation osimertinib at 6 months post-chemoradiation. Tolerating well. COPD stable. Plan: Continue osimertinib. Repeat CT every 3 months, brain MRI every 6 months.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 9 months shows stable right lower lobe post-treatment changes. No new nodules or lymphadenopathy. No distant metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 12 months shows right lower lobe residual stable at 2.5 cm. Post-radiation fibrosis. No new disease. Ongoing stable disease.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: One year post-chemoradiation, 12 months on consolidation osimertinib. Excellent disease control. ECOG 1, stable. Plan: Continue osimertinib indefinitely. Surveillance imaging per protocol.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
PET/CT at 15 months shows low-grade FDG uptake in the right lower lobe residual (SUV max 2.8), likely post-treatment changes. No FDG-avid disease elsewhere. No evidence of recurrence.

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 52, transrectal ultrasound-guided prostate biopsy showed adenocarcinoma, Gleason score 5+4=9 (Grade Group 5), involving 11 of 12 cores, with extensive perineural invasion and comedonecrosis.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 52, whole-body bone scan showed no definite metastatic disease; CT abdomen/pelvis showed a large prostatic mass with possible seminal vesicle invasion and a 1.4 cm right external iliac lymph node.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 52, patient was staged as cT3bN1M0 and was treated with combined modality therapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 53, PSA nadir was 0.1 ng/mL on ADT; serum testosterone was 8 ng/dL.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 55, after completing planned 2 years of ADT, leuprolide was continued due to high-risk features; PSA remained at 0.3 ng/mL.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 56, routine bone scan showed new uptake in the right posterior 8th rib and left sacral ala suspicious for metastatic disease.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 56, PSMA PET/CT confirmed PSMA-avid lesions in the right 8th rib, left sacral ala, and a previously undetected focus in the right femoral neck; SUVmax ranged from 8.2 to 14.6.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 56, PSA had risen to 4.8 ng/mL with testosterone of 15 ng/dL on continuous leuprolide, confirming metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 57, PSA declined to 1.2 ng/mL on enzalutamide; bone pain was well controlled.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 57, next-generation sequencing (FoundationOne CDx) on archival prostate biopsy tissue revealed BRCA2 splice site mutation (c.8488-1G>A), homozygous PTEN deletion, and SPOP F133V mutation; HRD score was elevated; tumor mutational burden 3 mutations/Mb; microsatellite stable.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 58, restaging PSMA PET/CT showed mixed response with decreased uptake at sacral lesion but new PSMA-avid focus in the L1 vertebral body and a 1.2 cm PSMA-avid left hepatic lobe lesion suspicious for visceral metastasis.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 58, PSA rose to 9.4 ng/mL on enzalutamide with new bone and hepatic metastases; enzalutamide discontinued.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 58, after 4 months on dose-reduced olaparib, PSA declined to 3.6 ng/mL; hepatic lesion was stable on imaging.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 59, CT abdomen/pelvis showed enlargement of the left hepatic lesion to 2.4 cm and a new 0.8 cm right hepatic lesion; bone disease was stable.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 59, PSA rose to 16.8 ng/mL with progressive hepatic metastases on olaparib; transition to docetaxel was planned.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
Biopsy of ascending colon mass demonstrated poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with areas of necrosis and high mitotic rate.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
Genomic testing showed KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, no PIK3CA mutation, TP53 splice site mutation, SMAD4 loss; microsatellite stable (MSS) with intact mismatch repair proteins; tumor mutational burden low (4 mutations/Mb).

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 66 revealed ascending colon mass with adjacent mesenteric lymphadenopathy, innumerable bilobar hepatic metastases (largest 7.2 cm replacing much of right lobe), and bilateral pulmonary metastases (largest 1.8 cm in right lower lobe); staged T4aN2bM1b.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 66, baseline CEA was 487 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 312 U/mL; high-volume metastatic disease with right-sided primary carrying poor prognosis; goals of care discussed.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 66, cardiology clearance obtained for systemic therapy; ejection fraction 55% by echocardiogram; bevacizumab considered acceptable given stable cardiac status.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
Restaging CT after 6 cycles showed stable disease in hepatic metastases with slight decrease in pulmonary nodules; CEA decreased to 198 ng/mL but remained markedly elevated.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 67, CA 19-9 decreased to 187 U/mL; considered best achievable response with ongoing FOLFOX-bevacizumab; plan to continue until progression or toxicity.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at age 67 after 14 total cycles (last 4 without oxaliplatin) showed slow progression of hepatic metastases (largest now 8.1 cm) and stable pulmonary nodules; CEA 267 ng/mL.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT after 4 cycles of FOLFIRI-bevacizumab showed continued slow progression; CEA 334 ng/mL; patient maintained ECOG 1 performance status.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 67, given progressive disease on two lines of therapy, discussed TAS-102 or regorafenib as third-line options; patient elected TAS-102 given hand-foot syndrome concerns with regorafenib.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 67, palliative care co-management initiated for pain control and symptom management; patient's goals remain quality of life with continued anti-cancer therapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 67, punch biopsy followed by wide excisional biopsy of the right scalp lesion revealed nodular melanoma with Breslow thickness of 6.2 mm, Clark level V, ulceration present, mitotic rate 12/mm2, extensive lymphovascular invasion, perineural invasion identified, and microsatellites present.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 67, palpable right cervical lymph node fine needle aspiration confirmed metastatic melanoma, positive for HMB-45 and SOX10.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 67, staging CT of chest, abdomen, and pelvis revealed multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 2.3 cm), three hepatic lesions (largest 3.8 cm in the right lobe), and bulky right cervical lymphadenopathy measuring 4.1 cm.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 67, MRI of the brain with gadolinium showed two enhancing lesions in the right frontal lobe (1.8 cm) and left parietal lobe (1.2 cm) consistent with brain metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 67, comprehensive genomic profiling of the primary tumor identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 55%), NRAS wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C228T), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, TP53 R248W mutation, high tumor mutational burden of 28 mutations per megabase, and PD-L1 combined positive score of 15.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 67, serum LDH was significantly elevated at 485 U/L, and the patient was classified as Stage IV M1d due to brain metastases with an ECOG performance status of 1.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 67, colitis resolved to grade 1 over 3 weeks with steroid taper, and the decision was made to continue nivolumab monotherapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 67, restaging CT at 12 weeks showed mixed response with reduction in pulmonary nodules but stable hepatic lesions, assessed as stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 68, MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect with decreased enhancement of both lesions and no new intracranial lesions.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 68, LDH trended down to 342 U/L suggesting partial biochemical response to therapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 68, restaging PET/CT at 6 months showed continued partial response in pulmonary metastases and stable hepatic disease with decreased FDG uptake.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 68, restaging CT at 9 months showed progression of hepatic metastases with new lesion in left hepatic lobe despite stable pulmonary disease.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 68, given hepatic progression on nivolumab monotherapy with confirmed BRAF V600E mutation, decision was made to switch to BRAF/MEK inhibitor combination therapy, and LDH rose to 410 U/L.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 68, restaging CT at 8 weeks on dabrafenib/trametinib showed significant response with greater than 50% reduction in hepatic metastases and continued stability of pulmonary disease.

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
CT-guided biopsy of left lower lobe mass reveals moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with acinar predominant pattern and focal signet ring features. TTF-1 positive, Napsin A positive, CK7 positive. Specimen submitted for comprehensive genomic profiling.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
NGS (Tempus xT) on left lower lobe biopsy. EGFR exon 19 deletion (p.L747_T751del) detected at VAF 44%. TP53 G266V mutation (VAF 38%). APC truncating mutation (VAF 15%). No ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MET, RET, HER2, or KRAS alterations. PD-L1 TPS 5%. TMB 3 mutations/Mb.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: Stage IIIB unresectable EGFR exon 19 deletion-positive adenocarcinoma with chest wall invasion and N2 disease. Plan: Concurrent chemoradiation per standard of care followed by consolidation osimertinib. Staging brain MRI.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT shows 4.5 cm left lower lobe mass abutting and invading the posterolateral chest wall with rib erosion. Ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy at stations 5 and 7 (largest 2.3 cm). No pleural effusion.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
PET/CT confirms FDG-avid left lower lobe mass (SUV max 11.8) and FDG-avid mediastinal nodes (SUV max 9.2). No distant FDG-avid disease.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
MRI brain negative for intracranial metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
Post-chemoradiation CT shows left lower lobe mass decreased from 4.5 cm to 2.2 cm. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy resolved. Patchy ground-glass opacities in left lower lobe consistent with radiation pneumonitis. No distant disease.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: Good response to chemoradiation. Radiation pneumonitis improving on steroid taper. Plan: Start consolidation osimertinib once steroid dose is below prednisone 10 mg daily to avoid confounding immune suppression. Monitor for pneumonitis flare.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 3 months on consolidation osimertinib shows left lower lobe residual 1.8 cm with fibrotic changes. Pneumonitis opacities resolved. No distant disease.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 6 months shows left lower lobe 1.6 cm. Post-radiation fibrosis. No recurrence.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 9 months stable. Left lower lobe residual 1.5 cm. No progression.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Assessment: Stable disease at 9 months on consolidation osimertinib. Pneumonitis resolved. Tolerating treatment well. Plan: Continue osimertinib. Surveillance imaging every 3 months.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT at 12 months stable. Left lower lobe 1.5 cm with fibrosis. No new lesions.

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 40, right breast core needle biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3, triple-negative phenotype except HER2 positive. ER negative (

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 40, bilateral diagnostic mammogram showed a 4.8 cm irregular mass in the right breast with associated pleomorphic calcifications, BIRADS 5. Left breast unremarkable.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 40, MRI breast confirmed a 5.1 cm enhancing mass with ipsilateral axillary lymphadenopathy. Multiple suspicious axillary nodes right.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 40, PET/CT staging showed hypermetabolic right breast mass (SUVmax 12.4), right axillary lymph nodes (SUVmax 8.2), and a solitary 1.5 cm left hepatic lobe lesion (SUVmax 6.1).

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 40, ultrasound-guided liver biopsy confirmed metastatic breast carcinoma, HER2 IHC 3+. Consistent with primary breast tumor.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 40, tumor genomic profiling (FoundationOne CDx) revealed TP53 splice site mutation, ERBB2 amplification (copy number 18), and CDK12 loss. TMB 6 mutations/Mb. MSS.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 41, restaging CT after 4 cycles of TCHP showed complete resolution of the liver lesion and decrease in primary breast mass to 2.1 cm. Axillary nodes decreased in size.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 41, excellent response to TCHP. Discussed surgical options for the breast given oligometastatic presentation with complete response in liver. Multidisciplinary tumor board recommended continuing systemic therapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 41, PET/CT showed complete metabolic response. No FDG-avid disease identified. Clinical complete response achieved.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 41, patient doing well on maintenance HP. ECOG 0. No significant toxicities. Counseled regarding fertility preservation options given young age and good prognosis.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 41, MRI brain screening negative for intracranial metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 70, transrectal ultrasound-guided prostate biopsy showed adenocarcinoma, Gleason score 5+5=10 (Grade Group 5), involving all 12 cores with extensive perineural invasion, lymphovascular invasion, and areas of comedonecrosis.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 70, bone scan revealed diffuse skeletal metastases involving the axial and appendicular skeleton including calvarium, multiple vertebrae, bilateral ribs, pelvis, and bilateral proximal femora; described as a superscan pattern.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 70, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed extensive sclerotic bone metastases, bilateral retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy (largest 3.2 cm), and two subcentimeter pulmonary nodules in the right lower lobe indeterminate for metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 70, after 3 months of combined therapy, PSA declined from 84 to 12.4 ng/mL; back pain improved significantly with ADT and radiation.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 71, PSA nadir was 4.2 ng/mL on goserelin plus abiraterone; testosterone was 11 ng/dL.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 72, restaging bone scan and CT showed stable diffuse bone disease and stable pulmonary nodules; retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy decreased to 1.8 cm.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 73, PSA rose to 18.6 ng/mL on abiraterone with testosterone of 16 ng/dL; castration-resistant progression confirmed.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 73, next-generation sequencing (Tempus xT) on fresh biopsy of right iliac bone metastasis revealed TP53 R175H and R248W biallelic mutations, RB1 loss, PTEN homozygous deletion, AR amplification and AR-V7 splice variant detected; no BRCA1/2 or HRD mutations; tumor mutational burden 8 mutations/Mb; microsatellite stable.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 73, CT-guided biopsy of right iliac bone metastasis showed high-grade adenocarcinoma with focal neuroendocrine differentiation; positive for PSA, PSAP, and focal synaptophysin staining by immunohistochemistry; Ki-67 proliferation index was 45%.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 74, CT showed enlargement of pulmonary nodules to 1.8 cm and 1.2 cm confirmed as metastatic disease, progressive bone metastases, and new 2.1 cm hepatic lesion; PSA was 42 ng/mL.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 77, PSA is 88 ng/mL on cabazitaxel with diffuse progressive disease; best supportive care and hospice referral were discussed given declining performance status (ECOG 3).

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 73, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 80% plasma cells with sheets replacing normal hematopoietic elements; histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138 with cytoplasmic IgA lambda restriction; CD56 negative, CD117 positive; Ki-67 proliferation index 35% suggesting aggressive biology.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 73, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate showed del(17p) in 40% of plasma cells; no t(4;14) or t(14;16); gain(1q21) in 65% of cells; R-ISS Stage III based on ISS III with elevated LDH at 345 U/L and high-risk del(17p); double-hit high-risk myeloma.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 73, serum protein electrophoresis showed M-spike of 4.6 g/dL in the beta-gamma bridging region; immunofixation confirmed IgA lambda monoclonal protein; serum free light chain lambda 520 mg/L, kappa 10 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 0.02; marked immune paresis with IgG 280 mg/dL and IgM 15 mg/dL.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 73, whole-body low-dose CT showed extensive lytic disease throughout the axial and appendicular skeleton including innumerable lesions in the skull, spine, pelvis, ribs, and long bones; pathologic fractures at T6, L1, and left 5th rib; large soft tissue mass arising from the right sacrum measuring 5.8 cm.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 73, initial management included aggressive IV hydration with normal saline at 200 mL/hour, calcitonin 4 IU/kg every 12 hours, denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously, and packed red blood cell transfusion of 2 units; calcium decreased from 13.8 to 11.2 mg/dL after 48 hours of treatment; nephrology consultation obtained for acute kidney injury.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 73, initiated VRd induction with dose adjustments for renal impairment; bortezomib 1.3 mg/m2 subcutaneously weekly, lenalidomide 5 mg due to creatinine clearance of 22 mL/min, dexamethasone 40 mg weekly; dexamethasone also serving as treatment for hypercalcemia.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 74, after 6 cycles of VRd, SPEP showed M-spike 0.8 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain 52 mg/L; classified as partial response; not a transplant candidate due to age, renal impairment, and ECOG 2; transitioned to maintenance with lenalidomide and dexamethasone.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 74, follow-up PET/CT showed decreased but persistent FDG activity in the sacral mass now measuring 3.5 cm with SUV 4.2; stable appearance of other skeletal lesions; no new disease sites; overall partial metabolic response.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 74, after 12 months of maintenance, SPEP showed rising M-spike to 2.1 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain increasing to 180 mg/L; confirmed progressive disease per IMWG criteria; discussed second-line options.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 75, after 4 cycles of DPd, M-spike decreased to 1.0 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain 68 mg/L; classified as partial response; treatment continued with plan to continue until progression; patient reports improved energy and pain control with ECOG improved to 1.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 34, left breast core biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3 (tubule score 3, nuclear grade 3, mitotic score 3). ER negative (

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 34, breast ultrasound (mammogram deferred due to pregnancy) showed a 3.8 cm hypoechoic irregular mass in the left upper outer quadrant with suspicious ipsilateral axillary lymph nodes.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 34, MRI breast (non-contrast protocol during pregnancy) confirmed 4.0 cm mass with axillary adenopathy.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 35, patient delivered a healthy infant at 37 weeks via planned cesarean section. Infant with normal birth weight, no complications.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 35, post-delivery staging CT and PET/CT showed no distant metastatic disease. Breast mass decreased to 1.5 cm after AC. No new sites of disease.

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 35, left mastectomy specimen: pathologic complete response. No residual invasive carcinoma. No residual DCIS. 0 of 18 axillary lymph nodes positive. Treatment bed with fibrosis and histiocytic reaction.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 35, completed all adjuvant therapy. Pathologic complete response achieved. Excellent prognosis. Surveillance plan: exam every 3 months, annual imaging. Not a candidate for endocrine therapy given ER-negative status. Contraception counseling provided.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 34, tumor profiling: ERBB2 amplification (copy number 16), TP53 Y220C, no additional actionable mutations. TMB 5 mutations/Mb. MSS.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 35, surveillance CT 6 months post-mastectomy showed no evidence of disease.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 66, patient noticed a firm lump in the left supraclavicular region. Evaluation led to the discovery of a right lower lobe lung mass with extensive lymphadenopathy. Patient is a retired construction worker with prior asbestos exposure in addition to smoking history.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 66, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast demonstrated a 4.8 x 4.3 cm mass in the right lower lobe extending to the mediastinum and encasing the right inferior pulmonary vein. Extensive bilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (right paratracheal 2.6 cm, subcarinal 3.1 cm, left aortopulmonary window 1.8 cm). Left supraclavicular lymph node 2.4 cm. Bilateral pleural thickening and calcified pleural plaques consistent with prior asbestos exposure. No hepatic, adrenal, or osseous metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 66, PET/CT showed FDG-avid right lower lobe mass (SUVmax 13.7), bulky bilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 10.2), and left supraclavicular lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 8.9). No distant metastatic disease beyond nodal stations.

Please correlate clinically.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 66, ultrasound-guided fine needle aspiration and core biopsy of left supraclavicular lymph node confirmed metastatic adenocarcinoma. Immunohistochemistry: TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A positive. CT-guided biopsy of right lower lobe mass confirmed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. PD-L1 TPS 35% (22C3 assay).

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 66, next-generation sequencing (FoundationOne CDx) of tumor specimen: EGFR wild-type, ALK rearrangement not detected, ROS1 rearrangement not detected, BRAF V600E not detected, KRAS G12C detected, TP53 splice site mutation detected, ATM loss detected. No currently actionable driver mutations. Tumor mutational burden 10.6 mut/Mb. Microsatellite stable.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 66, multidisciplinary tumor board: unresectable stage IIIB adenocarcinoma with extensive mediastinal and supraclavicular involvement. Disease volume too extensive for definitive radiation. Recommended systemic therapy with carboplatin/pemetrexed/pembrolizumab.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 66, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed partial response. Right lower lobe mass decreased to 3.2 x 2.8 cm. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy decreased. Left supraclavicular node decreased to 1.2 cm.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 67, restaging CT after 4 cycles of maintenance showed continued partial response. Right lower lobe mass 2.7 x 2.4 cm. Lymphadenopathy continuing to improve.

Please correlate clinically.
